title: Convert lidar to numpy signals

china-office.reason: Client-side lidar→numpy utility, not a distinct e2e skill — the floorplan run above consumes it. Validated by its own unit suite.

dimsim-spatial-memory.reason: Same — consumed by the mapping runs, validated by unit tests; nothing scenario-specific to show.

human-activity.reason: Scenario-agnostic utility; nothing activity-specific to exercise.

summary_html: A client-side utility, LidarPointCloudClient, that lets any script or skill running outside the module graph subscribe to live lidar over LCM and pull the accumulated cloud as a plain (N, 3) float32 numpy array — no hand-rolled subscribe/accumulate/vstack bookkeeping. Extracted from the floorplan demo's ad-hoc collector, which now uses it. New: a set of pure-numpy signal tools (signals.py) turns that raw array into the products four objectives need — measure the space (extents, ground_height), reactive clearance/safety (nearest_obstacle, clearance, region_is_clear), a portable map artifact (occupancy_grid, height_map), and coverage feedback (coverage_area) — with thin client wrappers so an outside-graph caller can ask the live sensor directly.

test_command.note: headless — synthetic scanner sweep; 35 unit tests

skills.name: measure_space()
skills.desc_html: Bounding box + dimensions (w × d × h) of the scanned space — "how big is this room?"

skills.name: nearest_obstacle_distance()
skills.desc_html: Horizontal distance from the robot to the closest point at body height — a quick "is anything close to me?"

skills.name: check_clearance(heading_deg, fov_deg)
skills.desc_html: Distance to the nearest point in a cone around a heading (default: straight ahead) — "is this direction clear?"

skills.name: coverage_report()
skills.desc_html: Points + frames accumulated and approximate floor area scanned — "how much have you seen?"

skills_note: the raw numpy substrate is a library API (LidarPointCloudClient + signals.py, consumed by item 1 and any outside-graph script); LidarSignalSkills exposes the four highest-value queries as MCP @skills, wired into unitree-go2-scene-memory-agentic so an agent can ask them in natural language

figures.alt: Left: step chart of snapshot() point count growing over 48 lidar messages to 35,962 points. Right: top-down view of the accumulated cloud forming a 6 by 4 meter room with a round pillar
figures.caption_html: The actual client accumulating 48 sector scans of a synthetic 6×4 m room (one scanner revolution). Left: snapshot() grows monotonically as /lidar chunks arrive. Right: the final (35,962 × 3) float32 array plotted top-down — walls and a 0.36 m pillar reconstructed, ready for plain numpy work. Regenerate: uv run python misc/skills_dashboard/scripts/gen_figures.py
figures.heading: What it does — real class fed a spinning synthetic scanner

how_it_works.title: Subscribe
how_it_works.html: Opens LCMTransport subscriptions to /lidar (per-scan chunks) and optionally /global_map (an already-accumulated map). Usable as a context manager: with LidarPointCloudClient() as client:.

how_it_works.title: Accumulate
how_it_works.html: Each /lidar message is converted with points_f32() and appended to a chunk list; empty messages are ignored. /global_map is cumulative upstream, so it's latest-wins rather than appended. All state is behind one lock.

how_it_works.title: Snapshot on demand
how_it_works.html: snapshot() returns a thread-safe vstack of buffered chunks + global map as (N, 3) float32 (or (0, 3) when nothing arrived). clear() drops the chunks but deliberately keeps the last global map.

how_it_works.title: Derive signals (pure numpy)
how_it_works.html: signals.py is stateless: every function takes an (N, 3) array and returns plain numpy/scalars (no LCM, open3d, or graph), so it runs in a script, notebook, or skill. Measure: extents() → bbox + room dimensions, ground_height(). Clearance/safety: nearest_obstacle(), clearance(heading, fov) (closest point in a cone ahead), region_is_clear(box) — an optional height_band ignores floor/ceiling so "nearest thing" means "at body height." Map artifact: occupancy_grid() (top-down (H, W) + origin + resolution), height_map() (2.5D max-z). Coverage: coverage_area() (scanned m²). Building blocks: crop, filter_height, voxel_downsample. Empty-cloud policy: measurement queries raise, distance queries return inf (nothing near = clear), grid/area return empty/zero.

how_it_works.title: Ask the live sensor
how_it_works.html: Thin LidarPointCloudClient wrappers apply the signal tools to the current snapshot, so an outside-graph caller asks the sensor directly: client.extents(), client.nearest_obstacle(height_band=…), client.clearance(heading), client.occupancy_grid(), client.coverage_summary() (points + messages + scanned area, with area_m2=None until anything is scanned).

scope_note_html: Scope note: single-message conversion (PointCloud2.points_f32() / .as_numpy()) and in-graph accumulation + occupancy rasterization already existed and are unchanged — this fills the gap for client-side consumers only.

test_descriptions.test_empty_snapshot_is_zero_by_three: No messages → well-formed (0, 3) float32 array, never an error
test_descriptions.test_lidar_chunks_accumulate: Successive /lidar scans stack into one growing cloud
test_descriptions.test_global_map_included_alongside_lidar: Snapshot merges the global map with buffered scans
test_descriptions.test_global_map_is_latest_wins: A newer /global_map replaces (not appends to) the old one
test_descriptions.test_clear_drops_lidar_chunks_but_keeps_: clear() resets scan buffer while preserving the map
test_descriptions.test_empty_pointcloud_message_is_ignored: Zero-point messages don't pollute the buffer or the count
test_descriptions.test_client_extents_over_snapshot: Client extents() reports per-axis span of everything scanned
test_descriptions.test_client_nearest_obstacle_and_clearan: Client nearest_obstacle()/clearance() read the live snapshot; a cone facing away returns inf
test_descriptions.test_client_coverage_summary_reports_non: coverage_summary() gives points/messages/area, with area None before any scan
test_descriptions.test_as_xyz_rejects_wrong_shape: Non-(N, 3) input is rejected rather than silently mis-processed
test_descriptions.test_crop_keeps_only_inside_box: crop() keeps only points inside the axis-aligned box
test_descriptions.test_filter_height_band: filter_height() keeps only points within the z band
test_descriptions.test_voxel_downsample_collapses_duplicat: voxel_downsample() collapses overlapping-sweep duplicates; finer voxels separate them
test_descriptions.test_extents_recovers_room_dimensions: Measure: extents() recovers a 6×4×2.4 m room's span and center
test_descriptions.test_extents_empty_raises: extents() on an empty cloud raises rather than returning garbage
test_descriptions.test_ground_height_finds_floor: ground_height() locates the floor at z≈0 via a low z-percentile
test_descriptions.test_nearest_obstacle_distance: Safety: nearest wall to the origin is recovered at the true distance
test_descriptions.test_nearest_obstacle_height_band_ignore: A height_band ignores the floor so the nearest body-height obstacle wins
test_descriptions.test_nearest_obstacle_empty_is_inf: No points → inf (nothing near = clear), never an error
test_descriptions.test_clearance_cone_direction: clearance() sees an obstacle in the facing cone but not one behind
test_descriptions.test_region_is_clear: region_is_clear() is True for an empty box, False when a point is inside
test_descriptions.test_occupancy_grid_shape_and_perimeter: Map: occupancy grid has the right shape/origin; perimeter occupied, interior free
test_descriptions.test_occupancy_grid_fixed_bounds_handles: Fixed bounds yield a correctly-sized empty grid on an empty cloud
test_descriptions.test_height_map_max_z_per_cell: Height map takes the max z per cell and is NaN where empty
test_descriptions.test_coverage_area_matches_occupied_cell: Coverage: a 2×2 m patch reports ≈4 m² scanned area
test_descriptions.test_coverage_area_empty_is_zero: Coverage of an empty cloud is 0.0
test_descriptions.test_measure_space_reports_dimensions: MCP skill: measure_space() reports the scanned space's width × depth × height
test_descriptions.test_measure_space_no_data: measure_space() says so cleanly before any lidar arrives
test_descriptions.test_nearest_obstacle_from_robot_pose: From the robot's pose, nearest-obstacle finds the wall and ignores the floor under it
test_descriptions.test_check_clearance_uses_robot_heading: check_clearance() sees an obstacle when facing it, reports clear when turned away
test_descriptions.test_coverage_report: coverage_report() goes from "nothing scanned" to points/frames/area after a scan
test_descriptions.test_lidar_snapshot_accumulates_numpy_cl: Skill-verification: a synthetic scanner sweep grows snapshot() into an (N, 3) float cloud spanning the room; saves the accumulation curve + top-down cloud for review

tests_note_html: the last row is from the skill-verification suite (dimos/skills/skill_verification), which drives the real client end-to-end and writes a review figure to test_outputs/ (indexed by REVIEW.md)

examples.pre_html: from dimos.mapping.pointclouds.live import LidarPointCloudClient with LidarPointCloudClient() as client: # /lidar + /global_map time.sleep(5.0) # let scans arrive pts = client.snapshot() # (N, 3) float32, world frame heights = pts[:, 2] # plain numpy from here on
examples.caption: the floorplan demo (item 1) consumes exactly this API for its collection stage

next_step_html: Validate the four MCP skills live/replay via the master VQA e2e (test_master_vqa*, whose question bank now includes item-3 measure/clearance/coverage questions), then close objective 6's loop by letting a coverage-driven explorer stop once coverage_report plateaus.

review_artifacts.label: lidar_accumulation.png
review_artifacts.note: left: growth curve rises then plateaus after one revolution; right: top-down cloud outlines the room + pillar
